title: erica viscaria subsp. pendula
intro: Erica viscaria subsp. pendula, also known as the Bell Heather, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the heath family, Ericaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been found to have medicinal properties such as antibacterial and antifungal activity. The health benefits of Erica viscaria subsp. pendula include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as having antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in skin care products due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
